questions.q: Discuss the theme of 'The Enemy' by Charlie Donlea. How does it relate to the human condition? (5 marks)
questions.answer: The theme of 'The Enemy' is centered around the concept of human nature and how people perceive each other. The novel explores the idea that people's appearances can be deceiving and that one should not judge others based on their physical appearance. This theme relates to the human condition as it highlights the complexities of human relationships and the importance of empathy and understanding. The novel also touches upon the idea of fear and how it can drive people to make irrational decisions. This theme is relevant to the human condition as it shows how people's fears can lead to conflict and division.
marking_scheme: Analysis of the theme's relation to the human condition (3 marks)

questions.q: Describe the character of Joe in 'The Enemy' by Charlie Donlea. Analyze his motivations and actions throughout the novel. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Joe is a complex character in the novel, portrayed as a rugged and mysterious individual. His motivations are driven by a desire for survival and revenge against those who wronged him. Throughout the novel, Joe's actions are guided by his primal instincts, which are shaped by his experiences in the wilderness. He is a product of his environment, and his actions are a reflection of his surroundings. Joe's character serves as a foil to the other characters in the novel, highlighting their weaknesses and vulnerabilities.
marking_scheme: Analysis of Joe's motivations and actions (3 marks)

questions.q: Explain the symbolism of the island in 'The Enemy' by Charlie Donlea. How does it relate to the themes of the novel? (5 marks)
questions.answer: The island in 'The Enemy' serves as a symbol of isolation and confinement. It represents a place where characters are trapped and forced to confront their inner demons. The island also symbolizes the idea of a 'wilderness' within human beings, where primal instincts and desires reside. The island's harsh environment serves as a metaphor for the characters' inner struggles, highlighting their vulnerabilities and weaknesses. The symbolism of the island relates to the themes of the novel, particularly the idea that people are capable of both good and evil.
marking_scheme: Explanation of the symbolism of the island (2 marks)
Analysis of the island's relation to the themes (3 marks)

questions.q: In 'The Enemy' by Charlie Donlea, calculate the distance Joe travels in the first 5 days after being stranded on the island. Assume he walks 10 km/day. (5 marks)
questions.answer:
Calculation: Distance = Speed × Time
Distance = 10 km/day × 5 days
Distance = 50 km

questions.q: In 'The Enemy' by Charlie Donlea, calculate the amount of food Joe has left after 10 days on the island. Assume he starts with 20 kg of food and eats 2 kg/day. (5 marks)
questions.answer:
Calculation: Food left = Initial food - Food consumed
Food left = 20 kg - (2 kg/day × 10 days)
Food left = 20 kg - 20 kg
Food left = 0 kg

questions.q: In 'The Enemy' by Charlie Donlea, calculate the number of days Joe will survive on the island if he only has 5 kg of food left and eats 1.5 kg/day. (5 marks)
questions.answer:
Calculation: Number of days = Food left / Food consumed per day
Number of days = 5 kg / 1.5 kg/day
Number of days = 3.33 days
Since Joe can't survive a fraction of a day, we round down to 3 days.
marking_scheme: Calculation of number of days (2 marks)
